data.title: China Nonferrous Mining Corporation: Revenue and profit rose on higher copper output; major projects and strong outlook support growth
data.description:
Revenue grew 11.7% to US$2,053.3 million, with profit attributable to owners rising to US$146.3 million as higher production and sales volumes offset cost increases. Major projects progressed, gearing rose to 60.4%, and management remains optimistic on copper and cobalt demand.

Original document: China Nonferrous Mining Corp. Ltd. [1258] Earnings Release — Mar. 29 2029

data.description:
Q2 2026 net income rose 23% year-over-year to $26.8M, with EPS up 25% to $0.90. Asset quality remained strong, noninterest income grew 15%, and efficiency improved. Loans increased 7.6% year-over-year, while capital and liquidity positions stayed robust.

data.title: Havas Shares Rise After Company Signals Momentum on New Contracts
Versus Systems regains Nasdaq equity compliance after $1.7M stock sale and $1.485M license revenue
data.description:
Versus Systems regained compliance with Nasdaq's $2.5M stockholders' equity requirement after issuing shares for $1.7M and recognizing $1.485M in license revenue.

Key Highlights:

  • Issued 1,310,969 common shares to ASPIS Cyber Technologies for $1,700,000, boosting cash to $1.36M pro forma.
  • Recognized $1,485,000 of revenue in Q2 2026 from a functional license under ACT's amended Technology License agreement.
  • Pro forma stockholders' equity improved to $3,403,606 as of June 30, 2026, exceeding Nasdaq's $2.5M threshold.
  • Nasdaq will continue to monitor compliance and may commence delisting proceedings if future reports do not evidence compliance.

Original SEC Filing: Versus Systems Inc. [ VS ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Southside Bancshares posts Q2 net income $26.8M, EPS $0.90; assets $8.76B
data.description:
Southside Bancshares reported second-quarter 2026 net income of $26.8 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.90, with total assets of $8.76 billion as of June 30, 2026. The company recorded a 23.0% year-over-year increase in quarterly net income and delivered a return on average assets of 1.23% and return on average tangible common equity of 16.09% for the quarter. Nonperforming assets remained low at 0.11% of total assets while loans rose 7.6% year-over-year to $4.95 billion.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income (Q2 2026): $26.8 million; up from $21.8 million in Q2 2025 (23.0% increase).
  • Earnings per diluted common share (Q2 2026): $0.90, versus $0.72 in Q2 2025.
  • Total assets (June 30, 2026): $8.76 billion; up from $8.34 billion at June 30, 2025.
  • Loans (June 30, 2026): $4.95 billion, an increase of $347.6 million (7.6%) year-over-year.
  • Selected margins and ratios (Q2 2026): net interest income $57.3 million; net interest margin 2.80% (GAAP) and 2.90% (FTE); efficiency ratio (FTE) 52.96%.

Business Highlights

  • Linked-quarter loan production was active with modest net loan growth of $3.4 million; production included increases in commercial owner-occupied loans (+$21.5M), municipal loans (+$20.5M) and commercial loans (+$10.6M).
  • Deposit composition shifted: total deposits at June 30, 2026 were $6.17 billion, down $705.1 million from March 31, 2026 largely due to a decline in brokered deposits (down $777.9M linked-quarter), while retail deposits increased $189.4 million year-over-year.
  • Funding costs fell year-to-date: cost of interest-bearing deposits decreased to 2.57% for the six months ended June 30, 2026 (down 26 basis points vs. prior year period); linked-quarter cost of total deposits decreased to 1.94%.
  • Asset quality remained strong: nonperforming assets were $9.8 million (0.11% of total assets) and classified loans totaled $260.1 million; allowance for loan losses totaled $45.6 million (0.92% of total loans).
  • Liquidity and capital: total available contingent liquidity was $1.99 billion (net of current borrowings); shareholders' equity was $882.5 million at quarter end with approximately 0.8 million shares remaining authorized for repurchase under the existing plan.

Original SEC Filing: SOUTHSIDE BANCSHARES INC [ SBSI ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Hyli awarded $42M ONR contract to build 2MW and 3MW power units under HELMUR
data.description:
Hyli announced a roughly $42 million ONR cost-plus-fixed-fee contract to design, build and deliver 2MW and 3MW power generation units.

Key Highlights:

  • Contract value ~ $42 million to support the HELMUR Megawatt Scale Power Generation Units program.
  • Hyli will design, develop, build, test and deliver 2MW and 3MW units, advancing core technology and alternatives.
  • Work includes reducing magnet supply chain risk and developing additive manufacturing processes to enhance capabilities.
  • Work performed primarily in Cedar Park, Texas, over a 36-month base period expected to complete by July 2029.

Original SEC Filing: Hyliion Holdings Corp. [ HYLN ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Healthy Extracts Secures $258,750 One-Year Convertible Note From Labrys Fund II, Nets $225,000
data.description:
Healthy Extracts entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement and issued a $258,750 one-year convertible promissory note to Labrys Fund II, carrying 10% interest earned upfront and a $33,750 original issue discount. The company received approximately $225,000 in net proceeds, minus expenses. The note is non-prepayable without holder consent and becomes convertible after 180 days at the lesser of $2.00 per share or 75% of the lowest closing bid over the prior 15 trading days. Monthly amortization payments of $36,964.28 begin January 18, 2027 for six months unless converted earlier.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement and one-year convertible promissory note
  • Counterparty: Labrys Fund II
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 17 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 1 year
  • Reason: Enhance liquidity and working capital flexibility

Original SEC Filing: HEALTHY EXTRACTS INC. [ HYEX ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Hashdex Nasdaq CME Crypto Index ETF Establishes Staking Framework, Adds Marex and JSCT Partners
data.description:
Hashdex Nasdaq CME Crypto Index ETF announced several material agreements to expand its crypto operations. The Trust amended its Sponsor Agreement to permit staking, aligning governance and income allocation to support a new staking program. It also executed an Authorized Participant Agreement with Marex Capital Markets to manage basket creations and redemptions, bolstering primary market liquidity. Additionally, the Trust entered a Master Purchase and Sale Agreement with JSCT to trade digital assets on a principal-to-principal basis, supporting portfolio execution and liquidity. Together, these steps aim to enhance income potential and operational flexibility.

Agreement 1: Hashdex Nasdaq CME Crypto Index ETF Amends Sponsor Pact to Launch Staking Program

  • Agreement type: Third Amendment to the Sponsor Agreement
  • Counterparty: Sponsor
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 23 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Enable staking and establish income allocation mechanics

Agreement 2: Hashdex Nasdaq CME Crypto Index ETF Adds Marex as Authorized Participant for Share Creations

  • Agreement type: Authorized Participant Agreement
  • Counterparty: Marex Capital Markets
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 23 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Enhance primary market liquidity and operational efficiency

Agreement 3: Hashdex Nasdaq CME Crypto Index ETF Signs Digital Asset Trading Pact With JSCT

  • Agreement type: Master Purchase and Sale Agreement for Digital Assets
  • Counterparty: JSCT
  • Signed / Effective: Jul 22 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Facilitate digital asset trading and liquidity management

Original SEC Filing: Hashdex Nasdaq CME Crypto Index ETF [ NCIQ ] - 8-K - Jul. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

REG - Lowland Inv. Co. - Result of Audit Tender
data.description:
RNS Number : 7989N Lowland Investment Co PLC 24 July 2026  

JANUS HENDERSON FUND MANAGEMENT UK LIMITED

LOWLAND INVESTMENT COMPANY PLC

LEGAL ENTITY IDENTIFIER: 2138008RHG5363FEHV19

24 July 2026

LOWLAND INVESTMENT COMPANY PLC

Result of Audit Tender

The Company today announces the result of a formal and competitive tender process for its external auditor, overseen by the Audit and Risk Committee. Following a detailed evaluation of each participating firm, the Audit and Risk Committee has recommended, and the Board has endorsed, the appointment of Johnston Carmichael LLP as the external auditor of the Company for the year ending 30 September 2027. A resolution to approve this appointment will be put to shareholders at the Company's 2027 Annual General Meeting.

Ernst & Young LLP, who have been the Company's auditor since 2016, will continue in the role until that time, and will therefore undertake the audit for the year ending 30 September 2026.

For further information please contact:

Janus Henderson Secretarial Services UK Limited

Corporate Secretary

Telephone: 020 7818 1818

Harriet Hall

PR Director, Investment Trusts

Janus Henderson Investors

Telephone: 020 7818 2919
